architecture_summary: Swin UNETR is a U-shaped 3D segmentation model that uses a hierarchical Swin transformer encoder with shifted-window self-attention and a CNN-based decoder connected by skip connections at multiple resolutions. It reformulates multi-modal MRI brain tumor segmentation as a sequence-to-sequence prediction problem for BraTS 2021.

training_details.compute_surface: trained on a DGX-1 cluster with 8 NVIDIA V100 GPUs

novel_contributions: We propose a novel segmentation model termed Swin UNet Transformers (Swin UNETR).

The task of 3D brain tumor semantic segmentation is reformulated as a sequence to sequence prediction problem wherein multi-modal input data is projected into a 1D sequence of embedding and used as an input to a hierarchical Swin transformer as the encoder.

The swin transformer encoder extracts features at five different resolutions by utilizing shifted windows for computing self-attention and is connected to an FCNN-based decoder at each resolution via skip connections.
The final result was obtained with an ensemble of 10 Swin UNETR models to improve the performance and achieve a better consensus for all predictions.

key_claims.claim: Swin UNETR is a novel U-shaped segmentation architecture that uses a hierarchical Swin transformer encoder and a CNN-based decoder with skip connections.
The model reformulates 3D brain tumor semantic segmentation as a sequence to sequence prediction problem using multi-modal MRI input projected into a 1D sequence of embedding.
Swin UNETR ranks among the top-performing approaches in the BraTS 2021 validation phase.
The encoder uses shifted windows for computing self-attention and extracts features at five different resolutions.
Swin UNETR outperforms all competing approaches across all 5 folds and on average for ET, WT and TC in the internal cross-validation benchmarks.
According to the official challenge results, the benchmarks are considered as one of the top-ranking methodologies during the validation phase.
The model demonstrates competitive performance in the BraTS 2021 testing phase.
